channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DOT Sec Sean Duffy finalizes $150M grant to build new port of entry on southern border
item.description: U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy announced Tuesday that his department had finalized an agreement for a $150 million grant to build a new entry port along the California border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon deputy chief of staff is second Hegseth advisor removed amid DOD leak probe
item.description: Pentagon deputy chief of staff Darin Selnick was removed from the Department of Defense headquarters on Tuesday, according to an official who confirmed the matter with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS agent who investigated Hunter Biden expected to be named IRS acting chief
item.description: An IRS whistleblower who testified publicly about investigations into Hunter Biden’s taxes is expected to be promoted to IRS acting commissioner, two sources familiar with the plan tell The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ten Commandments in classrooms 'grave concern' to Baptist pastor
item.description: A bill requiring the display of the Ten Commandments in public buildings and classrooms was signed into law in Arkansas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Shapiro arson attack: Religious bias suspected, DA says
item.description: "We do believe that there was a religious bias involved, and we're going to pursue that in our prosecution," Chardo said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump exempts nearly 70 coal plants from Biden-era rule on mercury and other toxic air pollution
item.description: The Trump administration has granted nearly 70 coal-fired power plants a two-year exemption from federal requirements to reduce emissions of toxic chemicals such as mercury, arsenic and benzene.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge orders federal agencies to release billions of dollars from two Biden-era initiatives
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday ordered the Trump administration to release billions of dollars meant to finance climate and infrastructure projects across the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Marshall man claims ICE detained him for protests over police killings
item.description: A Marshall man was detained by federal immigration authorities who claim he overstayed his student visa after it was revoked. However, he tells MPR News from the Kandiyohi County Jail that his arrest may have been politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel: Liberals 'viciously attacking comedians' are a 'big part of why Trump' won
item.description: "I think a lot of the outrage is completely manufactured, and it’s like, a lot of these people who are angry aren’t really angry," Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: A whistleblower’s disclosure details how DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data
item.description: A whistleblower tells Congress and NPR that DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data and hid its tracks. “None of that information should ever leave the agency,” said a former NLRB official.
item.dc:creator: Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Judge will order sworn testimony by Trump officials in case of wrongly deported man
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland will order sworn testimony by Trump officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia from an El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pritzker says he's undecided about testifying before Congressional panel
item.description: The US House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform requested testimony from Pritzker, other Democrats about sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: El Salvador's Bukele says he won’t return Abrego Garcia to US
item.description: President Nayib Bukele called Abrego Garcia “a terrorist” and said he had no power to return Abrego Garcia to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump urges Congress to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: President Donald Trump said Congress should “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers cut 6 new positions in Nevada Department of Public Safety budget request
item.description: Investigation backlogs and training needs within the Nevada Department of Public Safety are among the justifications for new positions in the state budget, but lawmakers said Wednesday there's just not enough money to pay for everything.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York bill aims to help homeless people and their pets stay together
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new state bill aims to expand access to co-sheltering, allowing homeless people and domestic abuse survivors to stay together with their pets during a housing crisis. Senate Bill S673, sponsored by New York state Sen. Monica R.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11
